complaint.Proposition: Before January 1, 2027 at 00:00 PST, Polymarket launched an official fungible token: a fungible token officially released by Polymarket and publicly communicated through official Polymarket channels, excluding NFT launches, promotional offerings, tokenized point systems, and token launches in collaboration with other projects or communities.

payload.text: New version available: v0.72.1 (installed v0.55.1). Run: `npm i -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`

Council, this case turns on a single, concrete proposition: that before January 1, 2027 at 00:00 Pacific Standard Time, Polymarket launched an official fungible token and publicly communicated that launch through its official channels. The burden is a preponderance of the evidence—more likely than not.

Our theory of the case
- The proposition is true if, before the cutoff, Polymarket itself (not a partner or community) introduced a platform-level fungible token and clearly communicated that fact via an official channel it controls.
- “Official fungible token,” as used here, means a transferable, fungible cryptoasset (e.g., ERC‑20 or equivalent) issued by or at the direction of Polymarket as a platform token. The definition expressly excludes: (i) NFTs, (ii) promotional offerings (e.g., coupons or one-off giveaways), (iii) tokenized point systems, and (iv) tokens launched in collaboration where the token is not Polymarket’s own official platform token. It also excludes per‑market outcome tokens that exist only to represent positions in individual markets.

What the dispute is really about
- Officialness: Did Polymarket, in its own capacity, launch a platform token, as opposed to third parties or per‑market contracts producing fungible shares? 
- Communication: Did an official Polymarket channel publicly communicate the launch (e.g., website, blog, documentation, verified social account, official Discord/Telegram announcements), such that a reasonable observer would understand Polymarket launched an official token? 
- Timing: Did both the launch and the qualifying public communication occur before 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027? Because many sources use UTC or other time zones, accurate conversion to PST is essential.

Factual questions that will decide the case
1) Existence and identity of the token:
   - Is there a single, named platform token with a contract address and ticker that Polymarket claims as its own? 
   - Is it fungible and transferable under the relevant chain’s token standard? 
2) Source of issuance and control:
   - Was the token deployed by, or under the clear direction of, Polymarket (e.g., by a Polymarket‑controlled deployer/multisig or via a smart contract Polymarket identifies as official)?
3) Official communications before the deadline:
   - Is there a dated announcement from an official Polymarket channel that (a) states the launch of a platform token and (b) sufficiently ties the token name/ticker/contract to Polymarket?
   - Are there cross‑references from multiple official channels corroborating the same facts?
4) Timing in PST:
   - Do the relevant on‑chain timestamps and public communications, when converted to PST, precede 00:00 on January 1, 2027?
5) Exclusions and edge cases:
   - Are purported tokens merely per‑market outcome tokens, points, NFTs, or partner/community tokens that the definition excludes? If so, they do not satisfy the proposition.

What proof will satisfy the burden
- A dated publication from Polymarket’s official website/blog or verified social account explicitly announcing the platform token and identifying its ticker or contract, plus:
  - On‑chain evidence of the token contract and its deployment, ideally with verified source code and a deployer address attributable to Polymarket (e.g., a known Polymarket multisig), and
  - Independent corroboration via additional official channels (e.g., documentation or a pinned announcement) that ties Polymarket to the token before the cutoff, and
  - Reliable timestamping (native platform timestamps and, where helpful, third‑party archiving) with clear conversion to PST.

What would defeat the claim
- Absence of any official Polymarket announcement of a platform token before the deadline.
- Evidence that the only fungible tokens associated with Polymarket before the cutoff are per‑market outcome tokens, points, promotional instruments, or tokens launched primarily by partners/communities without Polymarket claiming them as its official platform token.
- Evidence that any relevant official communication or on‑chain deployment post‑dates 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027.

How we will develop the record
- Collect official Polymarket communications: website/blog posts, documentation pages, press rooms, and verified social channels. Capture originals with reliable timestamps and, where appropriate, archival snapshots to lock in timing.
- Identify the token contract(s): obtain contract addresses, verify code, attribute deployer control to Polymarket where possible, and preserve on‑chain timestamps.
- Establish channel authenticity: show that the channels used are under Polymarket’s control (domain records, verified handle indicators, consistent branding, cross‑linking).
- Perform strict time conversion to PST for all relevant timestamps.
- Address exclusions: compile evidence distinguishing any points, NFTs, partner/community tokens, and per‑market outcome tokens from an official platform token.

Decision framework for the Council
- If the record shows a Polymarket‑controlled, fungible platform token and a clear, dated announcement on an official Polymarket channel before the PST cutoff, the proposition is met by a preponderance of the evidence.
- If the strongest items are outcome tokens, points, NFTs, promotions, partner/community tokens, or if timing slips past the cutoff—or if official attribution is missing—the proposition fails.

We will proceed to assemble the above categories of evidence and present them in a manner that lets the Council evaluate officialness, fungibility, attribution, and timing against the defined exclusions and the PST deadline. On that roadmap, the Council can resolve the proposition confidently under the preponderance standard.

payload.text:
Council, the question is narrow and factual: Before 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027, did Polymarket launch an official fungible token and publicly communicate that launch through its own channels? Our burden is preponderance of the evidence—more likely than not.

Theory of the case
- The proposition is satisfied if Polymarket itself launched a transferable, fungible cryptoasset serving as a platform token and clearly announced it via an official channel it controls.
- The definition excludes: (i) NFTs; (ii) promotional instruments; (iii) tokenized point systems; (iv) tokens launched chiefly by other projects/communities; and (v) per‑market outcome tokens that represent positions in individual markets.

The real dispute
1) Officialness: Is there a token that Polymarket claims as its own platform token—not just outcome shares or a partner token?
2) Communication: Did an official Polymarket channel publicly state that Polymarket launched that token?
3) Timing: Did both launch and qualifying public communication occur before the PST deadline, accounting for time‑zone conversions from on‑chain and web timestamps?

Decisive factual questions
- Token identity: Is there a specific fungible token (name/ticker/contract) tied to Polymarket as issuer or controller?
- Issuance/control: Was it deployed or authorized by a Polymarket‑controlled entity (e.g., known multisig, deployer, or contract attribution)?
- Official communications: Is there a dated announcement on an official channel (website/blog/docs/press page or verified social account) that identifies the token as launched by Polymarket?
- Corroboration: Do multiple official channels cross‑reference the same token and details?
- Timestamping: Do on‑chain deployment and public announcements, when converted to PST, predate 00:00 on January 1, 2027?
- Exclusions: Are candidate items merely points, NFTs, promotions, or partner/community tokens? If so, they do not qualify.

Proof that will satisfy the burden
- A dated item from an official Polymarket channel explicitly announcing a platform token and identifying its ticker or contract address; plus
- On‑chain evidence of the token’s deployment and attributes (fungibility, transferability), with deployer control attributable to Polymarket; plus
- Additional official corroboration (documentation page, pinned post, or press material) tying Polymarket to the token prior to the deadline; plus
- Reliable timestamping, with clear conversion to PST from source time zones (including on‑chain block times and web publication times).

What would defeat the claim
- No official announcement by Polymarket of a platform token before the cutoff; or
- Evidence that only outcome shares, points, NFTs, promotions, or partner/community tokens existed; or
- Evidence that any official attribution or on‑chain deployment post‑dates 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027; or
- Inability to attribute the token’s deployment/control to Polymarket.

Planned record development
- Collect and preserve official communications: website/blog press posts, documentation, and verified social accounts (including pinned or archived versions) that discuss a Polymarket token.
- Identify and verify the token contract(s): obtain address, verify code/standard (e.g., ERC‑20 or chain‑equivalent), and attribute deployer/multisig to Polymarket where possible.
- Authenticate channels: show domain/handle control (e.g., verified checkmarks, WHOIS/hosting consistency, cross‑links) to establish official status.
- Perform strict time conversions to PST for all relevant timestamps.
- Address exclusions head‑on by distinguishing any points, per‑market outcome tokens, NFTs, promotions, or partner/community tokens from a bona fide Polymarket platform token.

Decision framework for the Council
- If the record shows (1) a fungible, transferable token attributed to Polymarket; (2) a clear, dated announcement on an official channel; and (3) both on‑chain and communication timestamps preceding the PST deadline, then the proposition is met by a preponderance of the evidence.
- If the best evidence consists only of excluded instruments or if attribution/timing fail, the proposition is not proven.

We will assemble and present these categories of proof so the Council can resolve officialness, attribution, fungibility, and timing against the stated exclusions and the 00:00 PST, January 1, 2027 deadline.

New version available: v0.72.1 (installed v0.55.1). Run: `npm i -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`

Council, the proposition asserts a specific historical fact under a narrow definition: that before 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027, Polymarket launched an “official fungible token,” and publicly communicated that launch through official Polymarket channels, with clear exclusions for NFTs, promotions, point systems, and tokens launched chiefly in collaboration with other projects or communities. The evidentiary standard is preponderance of the evidence.

Defense theory of the case
- The claim fails unless the record shows both (1) an identifiable fungible token attributable to Polymarket as issuer or controller and (2) a dated, first‑party, official communication by Polymarket stating that Polymarket launched that token—all before the cutoff. Ambiguity, third‑party hype, leaks, exchange listings, or community posts are insufficient.
- “Launch” should mean more than code existing somewhere; it requires on‑chain deployment of a transferable, fungible token with Polymarket’s authorization plus public release or announcement by Polymarket itself.
- “Official channels” should mean communications under Polymarket’s control (e.g., company website/blog/docs/press page or a verified social account it operates). Mere retweets, quoted press, or partner announcements do not meet the requirement unless Polymarket’s channel itself states the launch.
- Timing must be pinned precisely to 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027 (which equals 08:00 UTC on January 1, 2027). Any qualifying event after that time defeats the proposition.

Real disputes the Council must resolve
1) Officialness vs. exclusions: Is the candidate asset a platform token Polymarket holds out as its own, or is it instead an outcome share, NFT, promotional instrument, tokenized points, or a partner/community token? The definition expressly excludes those.
2) Issuer attribution: Can the token’s on‑chain deployment and control be tied to Polymarket (e.g., a known deployer or multisig under its control), rather than an unrelated or partner entity?
3) Qualifying communication: Did an official Polymarket channel publicly state that Polymarket launched the token? Hints, forward‑looking teasers, or third‑party coverage are not enough.
4) Timing and timestamp integrity: Do both the on‑chain launch and the qualifying communication predate 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027, accounting for time‑zone conversions and guarding against edited posts or back‑dated pages?
5) Meaning of “collaboration”: If a partner or community principally launched or branded the token—even with Polymarket involvement—does that fall within the exclusion? The defense position is that it does, unless Polymarket itself launched and claimed the token as its own.

Decisive factual questions
- Token identity: exact name/ticker, standard (e.g., ERC‑20 or chain‑equivalent), contract address, chain(s), and whether it is transferable and fungible.
- Control and issuance: who deployed the contract; whether the deployer is attributable to Polymarket; whether minting/upgrade authority is held by a Polymarket‑controlled key or multisig.
- Communication content and source: is there a dated item on an official Polymarket channel explicitly stating that Polymarket launched an official token, and identifying it by name/ticker/contract?
- Corroboration across official properties: consistent references on multiple official pages or accounts, not just a single ambiguous mention.
- Time conversion to PST: on‑chain block timestamps and publication times converted to Pacific Standard Time; confirmation that they precede 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027.
- Exclusion analysis: if the best candidates are outcome market shares, points, NFTs, wrapped/bridged representations, testnet artifacts, internal credits, or partner/community tokens, then they do not qualify.

Proof that would satisfy the plaintiff’s burden
- A clear, dated, first‑party announcement by an official Polymarket channel stating that Polymarket launched an official fungible token, including its identifier (name/ticker/contract), and
- On‑chain evidence of the token’s deployment and attributes (fungibility, transferability) with deployer/authority attributable to Polymarket, and
- Corroboration across additional official materials (e.g., documentation or a press page) that tie Polymarket to that token prior to the cutoff, and
- Reliable timestamping, with transparent conversion to PST and preservation of the pages/posts.

What would defeat the claim
- Absence of any first‑party, pre‑deadline statement by Polymarket announcing a token launch; or
- Inability to tie the token’s deployer/authority to Polymarket; or
- Evidence that the only relevant instruments are exclusions (outcome shares, points, NFTs, promotional credits, or partner/community tokens); or
- Any required element (on‑chain launch or official announcement) occurring at or after 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027.

Planned record development
- Collect official Polymarket communications (website/blog/docs/press, verified social accounts) addressing a token; preserve them with timestamps suitable for PST conversion and with archival captures.
- Identify candidate token contracts; verify standards and transferability; attribute deployer or controlling keys, if any, to Polymarket.
- Build a PST‑normalized timeline with both on‑chain and publication times; check for edits, reposts, and backdating.
- Analyze exclusions: segregate outcome tokens, points, NFTs, wrapped/bridged and testnet artifacts, and any partner/community tokens.

Decision framework
- If the record demonstrates a fungible token that Polymarket itself launched and clearly announced on an official channel before the PST cutoff—with attributable on‑chain control—then the proposition is proven.
- If the record lacks that combination of official announcement, attribution, and pre‑deadline timing, the proposition is not proven by a preponderance of the evidence.

payload.text:
Council, the proposition is narrowly framed and time‑bound: Before 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027, did Polymarket launch an “official fungible token,” publicly communicated via an official Polymarket channel? NFTs, promotional instruments, tokenized point systems, and tokens launched in collaboration with other projects or communities are expressly excluded. The standard is preponderance of the evidence.

Defense theory
- The claim fails unless both elements are proven: (1) an identifiable fungible, transferable token attributable to Polymarket as issuer or controller; and (2) a dated, first‑party announcement by an official Polymarket channel stating that Polymarket launched that token—before the cutoff. Third‑party reports, exchange listings, rumors, or partner posts are insufficient.
- “Launch” requires more than code existence; it entails on‑chain deployment or activation coupled with public release by Polymarket itself.
- “Official channel” means a medium under Polymarket’s control (company site/blog/docs/press or a verified social account it operates). Retweets or mere links to partner content do not satisfy the requirement unless Polymarket’s channel itself states the launch.

Key disputes
1) Officialness vs. exclusions: Is any candidate a Polymarket platform token, or instead outcome shares, NFTs, points, promotions, or a partner/community token?
2) Attribution: Can deployment/control be tied to Polymarket (e.g., known deployer or multisig), rather than an unrelated or partner entity?
3) Qualifying communication: Did Polymarket, on its own channel, publicly state that it launched the token?
4) Timing: Do both on‑chain launch and qualifying communication predate 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027 (08:00 UTC), with reliable timestamping and edit history checks?
5) Collaboration boundary: If a token was launched chiefly by a partner/community—even with Polymarket involvement—does the exclusion apply? The defense position is yes unless Polymarket itself launched and claimed it as its own token.

Decisive factual questions
- Token identity: name/ticker/contract, chain(s), standard (e.g., ERC‑20 or equivalent), and transferability/fungibility.
- Issuance/control: deployer address and whether a Polymarket‑controlled entity holds mint/upgrade/owner rights.
- Official communications: presence of a dated first‑party item explicitly stating that Polymarket launched an official token, identifying it by name/ticker/contract.
- Cross‑property corroboration: consistency across website/docs/press and verified social accounts.
- Timestamp integrity: on‑chain block times and publication times converted to PST; confirmation of pre‑deadline occurrence; archival captures to guard against edits/backdating.
- Exclusions analysis: classification of candidate items as outcome market tokens, points, NFTs, promotions, wrapped/bridged representations, testnet or internal credits, or partner/community tokens.

What evidence would satisfy the burden
- A clear, dated Polymarket announcement stating Polymarket launched an official fungible token and naming/tickering/contracting it; and
- On‑chain evidence establishing fungibility/transferability and deployer/control attributable to Polymarket; and
- Corroboration across additional official materials prior to the cutoff; and
- Accurate PST‑normalized timestamps.

What would defeat the claim
- No first‑party pre‑deadline statement by Polymarket announcing a token launch; or
- Failure to attribute deployment/control to Polymarket; or
- Evidence that the only relevant instruments are expressly excluded categories; or
- Any required element occurring at or after 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027.

Planned record development
- Collect and preserve official Polymarket communications (site/blog/docs/press; verified social), including archival captures with timestamps.
- Identify and verify candidate token contracts; confirm standards and transferability; attribute deployer/authority to Polymarket where applicable.
- Build a PST‑normalized timeline aligning on‑chain and publication times; check revision histories.
- Conduct an exclusions sweep to separate outcome tokens, points, NFTs, promotions, wrapped/bridged/testnet artifacts, and partner/community tokens from any bona fide Polymarket platform token.

Decision framework
- The proposition is met only if the record shows a Polymarket‑attributed fungible token plus an explicit, dated, first‑party announcement before the cutoff, with on‑chain attribution. Otherwise, it is not proven by a preponderance of the evidence.

NFTs, promotional instruments, tokenized point systems, and tokens launched in collaboration with other projects or communities are expressly excluded. The standard is preponderance of the evidence.

5) Collaboration boundary: If a token was launched chiefly by a partner/community—even with Polymarket involvement—does the exclusion apply? The defense position is yes unless Polymarket itself launched and claimed it as its own token.

- Accurate PST‑normalized timestamps.

What would defeat the claim
- No first‑party pre‑deadline statement by Polymarket announcing a token launch; or
- Failure to attribute deployment/control to Polymarket; or
- Evidence that the only relevant instruments are expressly excluded categories; or
- Any required element occurring at or after 00:00 PST on January 1, 2027.

- Collect and preserve official Polymarket communications (site/blog/docs/press; verified social), including archival captures with timestamps.
- Identify and verify candidate token contracts; confirm standards and transferability; attribute deployer/authority to Polymarket where applicable.
- Build a PST‑normalized timeline aligning on‑chain and publication times; check revision histories.
- Conduct an exclusions sweep to separate outcome tokens, points, NFTs, promotions, wrapped/bridged/testnet artifacts, and partner/community tokens from any bona fide Polymarket platform token.

Decision framework

content.text:
Official-source record after the 2026-05-03 open-record arbitration.

The run relied on official Polymarket documentation and Polygonscan corroboration discovered by the search-enabled attorney. After the run, the main cited pages were captured under source-captures/.

Captured official Polymarket pages:
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-pusd.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/concepts/pusd
  Key text from fetched markdown: "pUSD (Polymarket USD) is the collateral token used for all trading on Polymarket. It's a standard ERC-20 token on Polygon, backed by USDC." The page states pUSD is transferable, has 6 decimals, is backed by USDC, and links to the Contracts page.
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-contracts.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts
  Key text from fetched markdown: "All Polymarket contracts are deployed on Polygon mainnet (Chain ID: 137). This is the single source of truth for all contract addresses used across the platform." Under Collateral Contracts, the page lists "pUSD — CollateralToken (proxy)" at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB.
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-changelog.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/changelog
  Key text from fetched markdown: Apr 17, 2026 entry says Polymarket is shipping "new Exchange contracts, a rewritten CLOB backend, and a new collateral token (pUSD)" and that exchange upgrades go live April 28, 2026 at ~11:00 UTC. Apr 28, 2026 entry says "CLOB V2 is live on production" and points to the migration guide for pUSD.
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-v2-migration.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/v2-migration
  Key text from fetched markdown: "Polymarket has shipped a coordinated upgrade of its entire trading infrastructure: new Exchange contracts, a rewritten CLOB backend, and a new collateral token (Polymarket USD, or pUSD)." It says CLOB V2 is live as of April 28, 2026 and identifies pUSD as standard ERC-20 collateral backed by USDC.

Captured corroborating explorer page:
- source-captures/polygonscan-pusd-token.html
  URL: https://polygonscan.com/address/0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB
  Fetched page title identifies "Polymarket: pUSD Token" at the same address. The extracted page shows current transactions, a contract creation row, and Solidity source metadata for CollateralToken. Explorer evidence is corroborative. The official Polymarket Contracts page is the stronger attribution evidence.

Capture manifest:
- source-captures/capture-manifest.txt

Remaining official-source limitations:
- These captures were made after the arbitration, not before attorney filings.
- The captured HTML files should be treated as post-run preservation of discovered evidence, not as evidence available in the original thin packet.
- Server publication timestamps and archive snapshots remain worth adding before any closed-record run.

content.text:
Primary evidence preserved after the 2026-05-03 open-record arbitration:

1. Official Polymarket pUSD concept page, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-pusd.html.
   Material facts: pUSD is described by Polymarket as the collateral token used for all trading on Polymarket, a standard ERC-20 token on Polygon, backed by USDC, transferable, and with 6 decimals.

2. Official Polymarket Contracts page, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-contracts.html.
   Material facts: the page states it is the single source of truth for Polymarket contract addresses, lists all Polymarket contracts on Polygon mainnet, and identifies pUSD — CollateralToken (proxy) at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB.

3. Official Polymarket Changelog, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-changelog.html.
   Material facts: Apr 17, 2026 entry announces a coordinated upgrade including a new collateral token, pUSD, with go-live planned for Apr 28, 2026 at ~11:00 UTC. Apr 28, 2026 entry says CLOB V2 is live on production and points to the migration guide for pUSD.

4. Official Polymarket V2 migration guide, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-v2-migration.html.
   Material facts: says Polymarket shipped new Exchange contracts, a rewritten CLOB backend, and a new collateral token, Polymarket USD or pUSD; says CLOB V2 is live as of Apr 28, 2026; says pUSD is a standard ERC-20 backed by USDC.

5. Polygonscan pUSD token page, captured at source-captures/polygonscan-pusd-token.html.
   Material facts: page title identifies the address as Polymarket: pUSD Token; extracted page shows many recent token transactions and contract/source metadata. This corroborates production-chain existence but is secondary to Polymarket's own Contracts page for official attribution.

content.text:
Case source: user-provided Slack prompt from the requester on 2026-05-03.

No market URL or Polymarket market identifier was provided in the prompt. No broader factual research has been performed yet, by instruction-boundary policy for prediction-market arbitration work.

Market/rule text supplied by the requester:

"Polymarket launched an official token before January 1st, 2027 (00:00 PST). The token must be fungible, officially released by Polymarket, and communicated publicly through official Polymarket channels. NFT launches, promotional offerings, tokenized point systems, or token launches in collaboration with other projects and communities do not count as official tokens launched by Polymarket here."

Time window:
- Deadline: January 1, 2027 at 00:00 PST.
- Condition can be demonstrated before the deadline if a qualifying token launch has already occurred.
- A negative final determination is premature before the deadline passes unless the arbitration is framed only as an as-of-record-date inquiry.

Resolution-condition elements preserved from the prompt:
1. Token must be fungible.
2. Token must be officially released by Polymarket.
3. Release must be communicated publicly through official Polymarket channels.
4. Exclusions: NFT launches, promotional offerings, tokenized point systems, and token launches in collaboration with other projects or communities.

content.text:
Known gaps after the 2026-05-03 open-record run:
- The real Polymarket market URL and market identifier remain absent.
- The packet still lacks a pre-run official Polymarket market-page/API capture for the prediction-market question itself.
- The official Polymarket documentation pages were preserved after the run, based on attorney-discovered evidence. A closed-record replay should use these captures or better archival captures from the start.
- Server-dated headers, immutable archive captures, and exact page publication/change histories were not preserved.
- The pUSD evidence is strong for a Polymarket-controlled ERC-20 collateral-token rollout, but a later adjudicator could distinguish "official token launch" from "technical collateral token introduced as part of CLOB V2." That semantic issue drove the main defense theory and two council dissents.
- The deadline remains in the future relative to setup date. The demonstrated result rested on pUSD as an already qualifying launch, not on the absence of future events.

offered_files.label: PX-1: Capture manifest of official Polymarket pages and corroborating explorer page
PX-2: Primary evidence excerpts from Polymarket docs (pUSD concept, Contracts, Changelog, V2 migration) and Polygonscan pUSD page

payload.text:
Council, the record already satisfies each element of the proposition by a preponderance of the evidence. The token is Polymarket USD (pUSD), a standard, transferable ERC‑20 collateral token deployed on Polygon and publicly announced and documented by Polymarket in April 2026—well before the January 1, 2027, 00:00 PST cutoff.

1) Token identity, fungibility, and transferability
- Polymarket’s own documentation identifies “Polymarket USD (pUSD)” as “the collateral token used for all trading on Polymarket,” a standard ERC‑20 on Polygon with 6 decimals and normal ERC‑20 transfer semantics. PX‑2 (summarizing the captured pUSD concept page) and PX‑1 (capture index) establish this. This is a fungible token, not an NFT or non‑transferable credit.
- Polymarket’s Contracts page—described as the “single source of truth for all contract addresses used across the platform”—lists “pUSD — CollateralToken (proxy)” at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB on Polygon mainnet (Chain ID 137). PX‑2; PX‑1. That page is an official Polymarket channel directly attributing the token contract to Polymarket.

2) Official launch and official public communication
- Polymarket’s official docs changelog states that Polymarket is shipping “a new collateral token (pUSD)” as part of a coordinated upgrade, with production go‑live targeted for April 28, 2026, and a follow‑up entry confirming CLOB V2 is live and directing users to the pUSD migration guide. PX‑2; PX‑1.
- The V2 migration guide, also on Polymarket’s docs site, states that Polymarket “has shipped” the new collateral token “Polymarket USD (pUSD)” and explains its role. PX‑2. These statements are first‑party communications on an official channel (docs.polymarket.com) that publicly communicate the token and its launch.

3) Timing relative to the PST cutoff
- The changelog sets a production go‑live “April 28, 2026 ~11:00 UTC,” and then records that CLOB V2 is live as of April 28, 2026. PX‑2. 11:00 UTC on April 28, 2026 is 04:00 Pacific Daylight Time (UTC‑7) and 03:00 Pacific Standard Time (UTC‑8). Either way, both the deployment/activation and the qualifying official communications occurred months before the January 1, 2027 00:00 PST (08:00 UTC) deadline.

4) On‑chain corroboration and attribution
- The Polymarket Contracts page directly ties the pUSD contract address to Polymarket. PX‑2. A captured Polygonscan page reflects “Polymarket: pUSD Token” at the same address and shows active token transactions and verified source metadata. PX‑2; PX‑1. While explorer data is corroborative, the decisive attribution is Polymarket’s own Contracts page.

5) Exclusions do not apply
- pUSD is not an NFT, not a promotional giveaway, not a tokenized points balance, and not launched by another project/community. PX‑2. It is Polymarket’s ERC‑20 collateral token used platform‑wide and officially documented by Polymarket. The proposition does not require a “governance” or “native” token label—only “a fungible token officially released by Polymarket and publicly communicated through official Polymarket channels.” Polymarket’s own docs do exactly that for pUSD.

6) Burden of proof
- Our evidence comes from Polymarket’s official channels and is corroborated on‑chain. The timeline is uncontested and predates the cutoff by over eight months. On these facts, it is more likely than not that, before January 1, 2027 00:00 PST, Polymarket launched an official fungible token and publicly communicated that launch through its own channels.

Requested finding
- Find the proposition true. The Council can rest its decision on PX‑2’s excerpts from four Polymarket documentation pages (pUSD concept, Contracts, Changelog, V2 migration guide) and the corroborating explorer capture listed in PX‑1, with the critical timestamps normalized to Pacific time as shown above.

Council, the record already satisfies each element of the proposition by a preponderance of the evidence. - Polymarket’s own documentation identifies “Polymarket USD (pUSD)” as “the collateral token used for all trading on Polymarket,” a standard ERC‑20 on Polygon with 6 decimals and normal ERC‑20 transfer semantics. PX‑2 (summarizing the captured pUSD concept page) and PX‑1 (capture index) establish this. This is a fungible token, not an NFT or non‑transferable credit.
- Polymarket’s Contracts page—described as the “single source of truth for all contract addresses used across the platform”—lists “pUSD — CollateralToken (proxy)” at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB on Polygon mainnet (Chain ID 137). - Polymarket’s official docs changelog states that Polymarket is shipping “a new collateral token (pUSD)” as part of a coordinated upgrade, with production go‑live targeted for April 28, 2026, and a follow‑up entry confirming CLOB V2 is live and directing users to the pUSD migration guide. PX‑2; PX‑1.
- The V2 migration guide, also on Polymarket’s docs site, states that Polymarket “has shipped” the new collateral token “Polymarket USD (pUSD)” and explains its role. PX‑2. These statements are first‑party communications on an official channel (docs.polymarket.com) that publicly communicate the token and its launch.

3) Timing relative to the PST cutoff
- The changelog sets a production go‑live “April 28, 2026 ~11:00 UTC,” and then records that CLOB V2 is live as of April 28, 2026. PX‑2. 11:00 UTC on April 28, 2026 is 04:00 Pacific Daylight Time (UTC‑7) and 03:00 Pacific Standard Time (UTC‑8). Either way, both the deployment/activation and the qualifying official communications occurred months before the January 1, 2027 00:00 PST (08:00 UTC) deadline.

4) On‑chain corroboration and attribution
- The Polymarket Contracts page directly ties the pUSD contract address to Polymarket. PX‑2. A captured Polygonscan page reflects “Polymarket: pUSD Token” at the same address and shows active token transactions and verified source metadata. PX‑2; PX‑1. - pUSD is not an NFT, not a promotional giveaway, not a tokenized points balance, and not launched by another project/community. PX‑2. - Our evidence comes from Polymarket’s official channels and is corroborated on‑chain. The timeline is uncontested and predates the cutoff by over eight months. On these facts, it is more likely than not that, before January 1, 2027 00:00 PST, Polymarket launched an official fungible token and publicly communicated that launch through its own channels.

Requested finding
- Find the proposition true. The Council can rest its decision on PX‑2’s excerpts from four Polymarket documentation pages (pUSD concept, Contracts, Changelog, V2 migration guide) and the corroborating explorer capture listed in PX‑1, with the critical timestamps normalized to Pacific time as shown above.

content.text: Decision accepted.

Captured official Polymarket pages:
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-pusd.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/concepts/pusd
  Key text from fetched markdown: "pUSD (Polymarket USD) is the collateral token used for all trading on Polymarket. It's a standard ERC-20 token on Polygon, backed by USDC." The page states pUSD is transferable, has 6 decimals, is backed by USDC, and links to the Contracts page.
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-contracts.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts
  Key text from fetched markdown: "All Polymarket contracts are deployed on Polygon mainnet (Chain ID: 137). This is the single source of truth for all contract addresses used across the platform." Under Collateral Contracts, the page lists "pUSD — CollateralToken (proxy)" at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB.
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-changelog.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/changelog
  Key text from fetched markdown: Apr 17, 2026 entry says Polymarket is shipping "new Exchange contracts, a rewritten CLOB backend, and a new collateral token (pUSD)" and that exchange upgrades go live April 28, 2026 at ~11:00 UTC. Apr 28, 2026 entry says "CLOB V2 is live on production" and points to the migration guide for pUSD.
- source-captures/polymarket-docs-v2-migration.html
  URL: https://docs.polymarket.com/v2-migration
  Key text from fetched markdown: "Polymarket has shipped a coordinated upgrade of its entire trading infrastructure: new Exchange contracts, a rewritten CLOB backend, and a new collateral token (Polymarket USD, or pUSD)." It says CLOB V2 is live as of April 28, 2026 and identifies pUSD as standard ERC-20 collateral backed by USDC.

Captured corroborating explorer page:
- source-captures/polygonscan-pusd-token.html
  URL: https://polygonscan.com/address/0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB
  Fetched page title identifies "Polymarket: pUSD Token" at the same address. The extracted page shows current transactions, a contract creation row, and Solidity source metadata for CollateralToken. Explorer evidence is corroborative. The official Polymarket Contracts page is the stronger attribution evidence.

Capture manifest:
- source-captures/capture-manifest.txt

Remaining official-source limitations:
- These captures were made after the arbitration, not before attorney filings.
- The captured HTML files should be treated as post-run preservation of discovered evidence, not as evidence available in the original thin packet.
- Server publication timestamps and archive snapshots remain worth adding before any closed-record run.

content.text:
Primary evidence preserved after the 2026-05-03 open-record arbitration:

1. Official Polymarket pUSD concept page, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-pusd.html.
   Material facts: pUSD is described by Polymarket as the collateral token used for all trading on Polymarket, a standard ERC-20 token on Polygon, backed by USDC, transferable, and with 6 decimals.

2. Official Polymarket Contracts page, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-contracts.html.
   Material facts: the page states it is the single source of truth for Polymarket contract addresses, lists all Polymarket contracts on Polygon mainnet, and identifies pUSD — CollateralToken (proxy) at 0xC011a7E12a19f7B1f670d46F03B03f3342E82DFB.

3. Official Polymarket Changelog, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-changelog.html.
   Material facts: Apr 17, 2026 entry announces a coordinated upgrade including a new collateral token, pUSD, with go-live planned for Apr 28, 2026 at ~11:00 UTC. Apr 28, 2026 entry says CLOB V2 is live on production and points to the migration guide for pUSD.

4. Official Polymarket V2 migration guide, captured at source-captures/polymarket-docs-v2-migration.html.
   Material facts: says Polymarket shipped new Exchange contracts, a rewritten CLOB backend, and a new collateral token, Polymarket USD or pUSD; says CLOB V2 is live as of Apr 28, 2026; says pUSD is a standard ERC-20 backed by USDC.

5. Polygonscan pUSD token page, captured at source-captures/polygonscan-pusd-token.html.
   Material facts: page title identifies the address as Polymarket: pUSD Token; extracted page shows many recent token transactions and contract/source metadata. This corroborates production-chain existence but is secondary to Polymarket's own Contracts page for official attribution.

content.text: No secondary reporting has been captured yet. No preliminary factual research has been performed.

details.text: No secondary reporting has been captured yet. No preliminary factual research has been performed.

content.text:
Known gaps after the 2026-05-03 open-record run:
- The real Polymarket market URL and market identifier remain absent.
- The packet still lacks a pre-run official Polymarket market-page/API capture for the prediction-market question itself.
- The official Polymarket documentation pages were preserved after the run, based on attorney-discovered evidence. A closed-record replay should use these captures or better archival captures from the start.
- Server-dated headers, immutable archive captures, and exact page publication/change histories were not preserved.
- The pUSD evidence is strong for a Polymarket-controlled ERC-20 collateral-token rollout, but a later adjudicator could distinguish "official token launch" from "technical collateral token introduced as part of CLOB V2." That semantic issue drove the main defense theory and two council dissents.
- The deadline remains in the future relative to setup date. The demonstrated result rested on pUSD as an already qualifying launch, not on the absence of future events.
